rationales.power: Khalid spoke over him using numbers Tariq had gathered, and Tariq 'just sat there,' leaving him publicly cut out of influence over his own work.
He says 'someone else holds the cards' and that being unable to move the ultrasound request forward leaves him feeling stuck and powerless.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he 'snapped at Faisal over homework' even though 'it had nothing to do with him,' showing harm to someone close because of displaced frustration.

rationales.achievement: He successfully reorganized the shift crisis and was told it was 'the calmest crisis she'd been through,' showing effective, recognized competence.
rationales.power: He pulled everyone together, set the frame for solving the staffing gap, and the ward ran well because he 'kept it steady' without having to force anyone.
He gave Amira 'full responsibility' and reflects that there are things he may not need to 'hold onto so tightly,' which means loosening his grip on control.
rationales.benevolence: He trusted Amira with the audit and praised her work 'in front of the team,' actively supporting a colleague's growth and recognition.

rationales.self_direction: He drafted his own protocol from patterns he had been tracking for months and openly pushed back when told to use the regional template instead.
rationales.power: Despite making the case, he is told 'the decision was made' and ends up implementing a protocol he does not believe in, with no real say over the outcome.
He corrected being introduced as only 'one of the senior nurses' and fixates on title and standing, showing strong concern for status and recognition.
He is talked over in the meeting, then 'sat there for the rest of the meeting barely saying a word,' emphasizing how little influence he feels he has in the room.
rationales.conformity: He says he is afraid of being seen as 'the angry nurse' and that if a doctor talks over you 'that's just how it is,' so he restrains himself to avoid becoming the problem.

rationales.achievement: He sought out a nursing leadership conference, handled his own shift coverage, and followed up on the form, showing active investment in professional advancement.
rationales.power: Even after doing the preparation, he can only wait on his department head's sign-off and says there is 'no reason for this to take a week' except that it is not his priority.

rationales.achievement: He spent 'two evenings' preparing a clear, documented staffing case with patient numbers, acuity trends, and incident reports, showing strong performance focus.
rationales.power: He concludes the real decisions happen through 'relationships, politics' and conversations he is 'not part of,' highlighting exclusion from influence.

rationales.achievement: He says 'I don't think I can stop preparing. That's who I am,' showing a continued commitment to doing the work thoroughly even after earlier disappointment.

rationales.power: She has led the committee for six years, but when Meena aunty questions her she 'stayed quiet' while no one defended her, leaving her authority publicly undercut.
rationales.conformity: She says defending herself would have looked exactly like what she was accused of, so she swallows the insult rather than risk social disapproval.

rationales.benevolence: She brings 'three containers of biryani' to a neighbor after surgery and says 'showing up when people need you' is what it is all about.
She spent 'an hour consoling' Priya after the birthday exclusion and, when asked, insists her focus is 'Priya, obviously.'

rationales.self_direction: She says 'I said yes before even thinking about it' and wanted to say 'let me check with Ravi first,' showing that she set aside her own choice.
rationales.conformity: 'What else could I say?' and 'the math is simple' frame the decision as obedience to expected family roles rather than personal preference.
rationales.benevolence: She agrees to host the in-laws for six weeks and immediately starts planning room changes and low-sodium meals around Appa's needs.

rationales.achievement: She lists concrete work like managing finances, sewing a recital costume until midnight, and proofreading seven essays, emphasizing sustained competence and effort.
rationales.benevolence: Her examples center on taking care of family members' needs, from finding Ravi's passport to supporting Priya's recital and Vikram's applications.

rationales.self_direction: She 'volunteered to step back' from leading Navratri planning, making an active choice about her own role instead of automatically keeping the responsibility.
rationales.power: Stepping away from vendor contracts and volunteer schedules means giving up the directing role she usually holds in committee work.

rationales.self_direction: She takes fifteen minutes alone on the balcony 'doing nothing,' carving out time for herself instead of staying absorbed in household demands.
rationales.hedonism: The quiet chai break with 'just me and the crows' is a small but deliberate moment of rest and enjoyment.

rationales.benevolence: She is proud that Vikram is 'kind' and 'notices people,' and she links that to years of family members showing up for one another.

rationales.conformity: She had already finished cleaning for the visit and immediately notices that 'Amma looked at the sheets,' showing how closely she tracks whether she is meeting household expectations for guests and elders.

rationales.self_direction: She says the kitchen is 'the one domain where I should have some say' yet still says nothing, showing her own preferences being overridden in her own home.
rationales.conformity: She tells herself 'she is seventy-two and she is a guest,' so she suppresses complaint in order to preserve expected respect and hospitality.
rationales.benevolence: Even while angry, she 'made everyone's favorite dinner anyway,' continuing to care for the household instead of acting on her frustration.

rationales.power: She insists 'someone has to keep track of things' and keeps reminding Priya because she feels responsible for keeping the household under control.
rationales.benevolence: Her pressure comes from concern that Priya will 'coast' and fail, and she frames letting that happen as something a mother should not do.

rationales.self_direction: She finally says 'this is my house and my family,' asserting her own judgment after two weeks of constant suggestions and corrections.
rationales.conformity: Rather than swallowing the comments to keep peace with an elder, she pushes back and accepts Ravi seeing her as 'too harsh.'
rationales.benevolence: In the response, she says she wants her children to remember that 'I showed up' and 'fed people,' grounding her efforts in care for close family.
